celebration: VOWEL WALL COMPLETE — the mid-year festival. A · E · I · O · U all lit.

songs.title: U-U-Up!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
U! U! U-u-u!
(uh… uh… uh…)

[Hook]
U! U! U-u-u!
What begins with U?
U-u-up! U-u-up!
It's up! (it's up!)

[Verse 2]
U! U! U-u-u!
What begins with U?
U-u-umbrella! U-u-umbrella!
It's an umbrella! (umbrella!)

[Verse 3]
U! U! U-u-u!
What begins with U?
U-u-under! U-u-under!
It's under! (down under!)

[Verse 4]
U! U! U-u-u!
What begins with U?
U-u-uncle! U-u-uncle!
It's my uncle! (an uncle!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not U!
(uh… uh… uh…)

[Outro — the vowels are complete]
A! E! I! O! U!
The wall is full! (A-E-I-O-U!)
U-u-up! Run up, pup!
U! U! U-u-u!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, kids choir chant hook, festival energy, celebratory horns stab on the vowel roll-call, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The U sound is the SHORT vowel /u/ as in 'up' and 'cup' — a low open 'uh', NEVER the letter name 'you' and NEVER the long u of 'unicorn'. THIS IS THE MID-YEAR SUMMIT: the outro is the five-vowel roll-call — A E I O U shouted in order, the whole wall lit — so let the beat swell and add a bright horn stab under 'The wall is full!'. Every short vowel the class owns lives in that line. Answer frames reuse Week 1.

songs.title: Run Up, Pup!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(run… run… run…)

[Hook]
Run up, pup! Run up, pup!
Where did the pup run up?
Up the hut! (up the hut!)
Run up, up, up!

[Verse 2]
Run up, pup! Run up, pup!
Where did the pup run up?
Up the bus! (up the bus!)
Run up, up, up!

[Verse 3]
Run up, pup! Run up, pup!
Where did the pup run up?
Up in the sun! (in the sun!)
Run up, up, up!

[Verse 4 — the whole cast]
Mud on the pup! Mud on the cat!
Mud on the dog! Mud on the rat!
Run up, pup! (run, run, run!)
Run up, up, up!

[Outro — the festival]
A! E! I! O! U!
Up on top… a potato!
(PO-TA-TO!)
Run up, pup! Run up, up, up!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, kids choir chant hook, festival energy, celebratory horns, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: ONE frame: 'Run up the ___!' with the place swapping (hut, bus, sun). Pup joins the cast this week — his debut is the mid-year party. Verse 4 stacks the ENTIRE cast in the mud; the outro reprises the vowel roll-call from the sound song so the two songs lock together into one festival. Potato at the summit, once, loud. 'down' is oral only.

spreads.artNote: A tiny bouncing puppy, ears flapping, pure joy. Pup joins the cast — the little one, the baby of the crew. The vowel wall glows A·E·I·O·U in the background, all five lit gold.
The pup tearing forward, tongue out, the Dog cheering him on behind. A festival banner strung overhead.
The pup scrambling up a grassy hill, determined, the sun rising above.

spreads.text: The pup ran up the hut.
spreads.artNote: The pup somehow on top of a little hut, delighted, wobbling. Absurd escalation.

spreads.text: The pup ran up the bus!
spreads.artNote: The pup on the roof of a toy bus, ears streaming, the cast waving from below.
The pup leaping toward a huge golden sun, silhouette, triumphant. The five glowing vowels arc across the sky like bunting.

spreads.text: The pup got in the mud.
spreads.artNote: Splat — the pup landed in a mud puddle, only eyes and a grin visible. The turn.

spreads.text: Mud on the pup! Mud on the cat! Mud on the dog!
spreads.artNote: THE PARTY: the whole cast — pup, cat, dog, rat, ant, Segina, Sam — splattered with mud, laughing, a full-cast festival panel.
The cast at the hilltop under the five glowing vowels A·E·I·O·U, confetti, the mid-year celebration in full. The pup at the front, tiny and triumphant.
At the very top of the hill sits the POTATO, wearing a tiny party hat and its round glasses, presiding over the festival. No noun printed — the whole class SHOUTS it.

sentences: The pup is in the mud.
extras: 🎉🎉 VOWEL WALL COMPLETE — the MID-YEAR FESTIVAL. Light the final U (blue letter, gold star). All five vowels now lit: A·E·I·O·U. Whole-class vowel roll-call, the festival song, confetti — this is the biggest celebration of the year so far
Short-vowel sort with ALL FIVE vowels: cat/pet/pit/pot/cup — one word per vowel, the class hears the whole set they now own
Moveable alphabet: build p-u-p, s-u-n, c-u-p, r-u-n — then run a word through every vowel: pat/pet/pit/pot/put to hear U take its seat
Pup joins the cast — welcome-the-Pup circle, the little one; the mid-year cast is complete: Segina, Sam, Cat, Ant, Dog, Rat, Pup
Half-year reading check: every child reads a book from Weeks 4–16 aloud to the cast — a keepsake certificate for the mid-year milestone

teacherFocus: THE MID-YEAR SUMMIT. U is the last vowel — when it lights, the whole wall glows A·E·I·O·U and the class owns every short vowel in English. Make this a FESTIVAL: the vowel roll-call, the festival song (the sound and word songs lock into one), confetti, a half-year reading check where every child reads a Weeks 4–16 book aloud to the cast. Teach the short /u/ of 'up' and 'cup' (low 'uh', never 'you'), blend p-u-p, s-u-n, r-u-n, c-u-p, and run a word through all five vowels: pat/pet/pit/pot/put. Pup joins — the little one — and the mid-year cast is complete.
